title: organization — compliance evidence
description: Organization structure changes are compliance-critical because they reveal a customer's org restructuring — reparenting, default flips, and archives are audit-material for SOC 2 CC6.3 and can be evidence in regulatory disputes about who controlled which asset when.

controls.evidence: Organization rows carry only structural + branding + contact metadata. No individual PII. `contact_person_name` (string) is the ONE PII-adjacent field; it is a designated contact for the org, not a member id, and is classified `internal` in `data-classes.json`.
TenantErased cascades hard-delete via FK. Retention job PurgeArchivedOrganizationsJob hard-deletes rows past the 730-day archive hold IF no descendants and no branch references.

Every Organization mutation is audit-logged via `owen-it/laravel-auditing` on the Organization model. Reparent + default-flip carry the before/after `tree_path` and `is_default` snapshots for full traceability.

Reparenting an Organization moves every descendant + every downstream scoped row (Branches, Teams, Facilities) under a new access-cascade root. The event carries `tree_path.before` + `tree_path.after` + `descendant_count` + `branch_count` so an auditor can reconstruct exactly which resources changed access boundary at what timestamp.

Two independent invariants monitored: (1) exactly one is_default per tenant — reconciled nightly + monitored via the `stackra.organization.default_invariant.violations` metric; (2) tree_path consistency across parent_id — monitored via `stackra.organization.tree_path_drift.count` after every RebuildTreePathsJob.

Structural changes (create + reparent + archive) are notifiable to tenant admins (see `notifications.json`). The audit event fires ShouldDispatchAfterCommit so the notification never lands on a rolled-back write.

Organization is a scope-node **entity** (per `hierarchy.md` §5) — every access decision that cascades through the tenant tree walks its `tree_path`. Structural changes are treated as security-relevant because they change the effective access set for descendants.
